description: Data persistence, SaveGame, DataTables, SQLite, cloud saves, and data management in UE5

entries.title: SaveGame Not Persisting / Data Lost Between Sessions
entries.description: USaveGame data is not saved properly or returns null/empty when loading in a new session.
entries.rootCause: The save slot name is different between save and load, the SaveGame object's UPROPERTY fields aren't set before saving, or the SaveGame class has been restructured (renamed properties) breaking deserialization.
solution: Use consistent slot names: define as a constant string
Mark ALL properties you want saved with UPROPERTY(SaveGame) or just UPROPERTY()
Use UGameplayStatics::SaveGameToSlot() and LoadGameFromSlot()
Check the return value of LoadGameFromSlot - it returns nullptr if no save exists
For the first run, create a fresh save with default values if LoadGameFromSlot returns null
If you rename properties, old saves will lose that data - use Serialize() override for migration

entries.title: DataTable Not Loading / Row Not Found
entries.description: DataTable->FindRow() returns nullptr, the DataTable appears empty at runtime, or CSV import fails.
entries.rootCause: The row name doesn't match (case-sensitive), the row struct type doesn't match the DataTable's row struct, the DataTable asset isn't loaded (soft reference not resolved), or the CSV format doesn't match the struct fields.
solution: Row names are case-sensitive - verify exact match with the DataTable editor
Ensure FindRow<T> template parameter matches the DataTable's row struct exactly
If using soft references to DataTables, call LoadSynchronous() before accessing
For CSV import: first column must be 'Name' (the row name), subsequent columns must match UPROPERTY names exactly
Check the Output Log during import for field mismatch warnings
In packaged builds, ensure the DataTable is included in cooked content (referenced by a map or in additional directories)

entries.title: SQLite Integration Issues
entries.description: Integrating SQLite for local database storage in UE5 projects encounters build or runtime errors.
entries.rootCause: UE5 includes a SQLiteCore module but it's not well documented. Third-party SQLite plugins may conflict. The database file path must be writable (not inside the read-only pak file in packaged builds).
solution: Add 'SQLiteCore' to your Build.cs for UE5's built-in SQLite support
Use FSQLiteDatabase class from the SQLiteCore module
Store the database file in FPaths::ProjectSavedDir() or FPaths::ProjectPersistentDownloadDir() for writable access
In packaged builds, use FPlatformMisc::GamePersistentDownloadDir() for platform-appropriate writable path
Handle database migrations: check schema version on startup and upgrade if needed
Always close database connections when done and handle errors gracefully

entries.title: Cloud Save / Platform Save System Issues
entries.description: Cloud saves (Steam Cloud, console platform saves) don't sync correctly, cause conflicts, or lose data.
entries.rootCause: Cloud save systems sync files asynchronously. If the game reads the save before the cloud sync completes, it uses stale local data. Save conflicts occur when the same slot is modified on multiple devices.
solution: Wait for the Online Subsystem's cloud sync to complete before loading saves
For Steam Cloud: configure auto-cloud paths in the Steamworks partner portal, not in-engine
Implement save versioning: include a version number in your save data to handle format changes
Handle conflicts by showing a UI: 'Use local save or cloud save?'
Use timestamps in saves to determine which is newer for automatic conflict resolution
For console platforms (PS5, Xbox), use the platform-specific save APIs via the Online Subsystem

entries.title: Data Asset vs DataTable: When to Use Which
entries.description: Confusion about whether to use DataTables, DataAssets, PrimaryDataAssets, or config files for game data.
entries.rootCause: UE5 offers multiple data management systems. Choosing the wrong one leads to maintenance headaches, memory issues, or difficulty iterating on content.
solution: DataTables: best for large collections of similar data (item databases, loot tables, dialogue). Easy CSV import/export for designers
UDataAsset: best for individual complex data objects (character ability definitions, quest definitions). Can reference other assets directly
UPrimaryDataAsset: extends UDataAsset with Asset Manager support. Use for assets that need async loading, bundling, or runtime discovery
Config files (.ini): use for settings that change between environments (server URLs, feature flags). Not for gameplay data
SaveGame objects: use ONLY for runtime player state that persists between sessions
CurveTable: specialized DataTable for float curves (damage falloff, XP progression)

entries.title: Serialization Errors / Save File Corruption
entries.description: Save files become unreadable after code changes (struct modifications, property renaming, class restructuring).
entries.rootCause: UE5's serialization uses property names and types to match saved data to struct fields. If you rename, remove, or change the type of a UPROPERTY, old saves can't deserialize that field. Adding new fields is safe (they get default values).
solution: Add a version number to your save data and increment it with structural changes
Override Serialize() in your USaveGame to implement manual migration between versions
Use Core Redirects for renamed properties: PropertyRedirects in DefaultEngine.ini
NEVER remove fields from save structs - deprecate them and add new fields instead
Adding new UPROPERTY fields is safe - they initialize to default values when loading old saves
Test save/load compatibility by keeping a set of test saves from each version

entries.title: Struct/Enum Changes Breaking Existing Data
entries.description: Modifying a USTRUCT or UENUM that is used in saved data, DataTables, or Blueprint defaults causes data loss or incorrect values.
entries.rootCause: UENUMs are serialized by name (safe to reorder) but removing/renaming values breaks existing data. USTRUCTs with renamed/removed fields lose those values on deserialization. Blueprint defaults reference property names directly.
solution: For UENUMs: never remove values - mark them with UMETA(Hidden) instead. Renaming is safe if you add a Core Redirect
For USTRUCTs: add new fields (safe), never remove old ones from serialized structs
Use UMETA(DisplayName) to change how an enum value appears without renaming it
Set up Core Redirects for any renamed enum values or struct properties
When restructuring is unavoidable, write a migration commandlet or editor utility to convert old data

entries.title: Async Save/Load for Large Data Sets
entries.description: Saving or loading large amounts of data causes a noticeable frame hitch or freeze.
entries.rootCause: UGameplayStatics::SaveGameToSlot/LoadGameFromSlot are synchronous and run on the game thread. Large save files (many actors, complex world state) can take hundreds of milliseconds to serialize/deserialize.
solution: Use UGameplayStatics::AsyncSaveGameToSlot() and AsyncLoadGameFromSlot() for non-blocking saves
Show a save indicator UI while async save is in progress
For very large worlds, save in chunks: serialize regions independently
Compress save data if file size is the bottleneck
Autosave on a timer during non-critical gameplay moments (not during combat/cutscenes)
Profile save time: measure how long serialization takes and optimize the largest contributors
